title: The entropy formula for the Ricci flow and its geometric applications
abstract: We present a monotonic expression for the Ricci flow, valid in all dimensions and without curvature assumptions.
ai_proof_review:
## Setup

The note introduces an entropy-like functional $\mathcal{F}$ on the space of Riemannian metrics modulo diffeomorphism and rescaling, together with its first variation under Ricci flow. The monotonicity argument hinges on a weighted-Laplacian identity that, once $\mathcal{F}$ is interpreted as a Lagrangian, behaves like a Bochner formula.

## Lemma 2.1

The claim is monotonicity of $\mathcal{F}$ along Ricci flow. The proof computes $\frac{d\mathcal{F}}{dt}$ in terms of $|\text{Ric} + \nabla^2 f|^2$, which is manifestly non-negative. The step is correct; the only subtlety is the choice of normalisation that absorbs the parabolic rescaling, which the paper handles cleanly.

## Overall

The argument is short, dense, and entirely correct. Several constants are absorbed into definitions in a way that makes the exposition compressed but not opaque. A careful reader needs to keep track of the convention $\partial_t g = -2\text{Ric}$; under the other sign convention several formulae flip.
